title: Acetosella lasiandra

Acetosella lasiandra is also a member of the genus Acetosella. The species is native to New Zealand and is commonly known as the wood sorrel or native wood sorrel. It is a perennial herb with leaves that contain a high concentration of oxalic acid.

intro:
Unfortunately, there is no information available on the plant "Acetosella lasiandra". However, Acetosella is a genus of plants in the family Oxalidaceae. 

If we assume that you might be referring to Acetosella (also known as Oxalis or Wood-sorrel), here's a paragraph about it:

 Acetosella (or Oxalis) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Oxalidaceae, commonly known as wood sorrel, with over 800 species, characterized by their heart-shaped leaves and white, yellow, or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Acetosella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and oxalic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acetosella include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried leaves and flowers used in culinary and medicinal applications.
